title: RhoDyn: dynamic residence-state analysis for live-cell signaling and perturbation data
description: RhoDyn is a standalone Python toolkit for residence-window scoring, bounded-coupling decisions, reserve-like buffering summaries, stochastic timing utilities, reduced-architecture comparison, reproducible reports, a command-line interface, a backend service, and a contract-bound browser workbench for live-cell signaling and perturbation data. The software is independent from the RhoA/microglia manuscript; manuscript materials are optional reference use cases, not runtime dependencies.
notes: Prepared archive metadata for the RhoDyn v0.1.0 software release. The published version DOI is 10.5281/zenodo.21036616 and the concept DOI is 10.5281/zenodo.21036615. The archive excludes raw microscopy, manuscript-private files, credentials, local paths, and generated debug outputs. The RhoA/microglia manuscript materials are optional reference use cases and are not required for normal software operation.
